2606.23797.title: From Task-Guided Conversational Graphs to Goal-Oriented Dialogue Runtimes
2606.23797.paper_summary: Graph and multi-agent orchestration frameworks make production large language model (LLM) workflows practical, but they do not by themselves solve conversational continuity when users maintain several interdependent objectives. This conceptual systems paper focuses on the high-complexity end of that design space, where goals can be suspended, resumed, revised, and invalidated by actions in other goals. We introduce the Goal-Oriented Dialogue Runtime (GODR), a framework-neutral design pattern that treats goals, task frames, lifecycle state, invalidation rules, and resumption contracts as first-class runtime objects while delegating bounded execution to graph runtimes, agents, tools, or application programming interfaces (APIs). GODR is not proposed as a replacement for workflow graphs in simple guided processes; it is intended for complex, multi-domain, interruptible conversations where objective continuity cannot be recovered reliably from agent identity, chat history, or execution-graph position alone. The paper formalizes the problem, proposes runtime objects and architecture-selection criteria, and frames evaluation as an agenda for future empirical validation rather than as a measured performance claim.
2606.23797.paper_summary_zh: 圖形和多代理協調框架使大型語言模型（LLM）工作流程的生產變得實用，但它們本身並不能解決當用戶維持多個相互依賴的目標時的對話連貫性。這篇概念系統論文專注於該設計空間的高複雜度端，在這裡目標可以被暫停、恢復、修訂以及因其他目標的行動而失效。我們介紹了目標導向對話運行時（GODR），這是一種框架中立的設計模式，將目標、任務框架、生命周期狀態、失效規則和恢復合同視為一級運行時對象，同時將有界執行委派給圖形運行時、代理、工具或應用程式介面（APIs）。GODR並不是作為簡單引導過程中工作流圖的替代品提出的；它旨在用於複雜的、多領域的、可中斷的對話，在這種情況下，目標的連貫性無法僅依賴代理身份、聊天歷史或執行圖位置來可靠地恢復。本文正式化了這一問題，提出了運行時對象和架構選擇標準，並將評估框架定義為未來實證驗證的議程，而不是作為一個衡量的性能主張。
2606.23797.author: Mariano Garralda-Barrio et.al.
